text:
The cited title does not match the actual paper title. The cited reference uses "extra training" but the actual paper title from multiple authoritative sources uses "increasing inference time." Let me verify the authors and year to confirm this is the same paper.
EXPLANATION: The paper exists and was published at ICML 2022 with the correct authors, but the cited title contains a substantive error. The actual paper title is "Model soups: averaging weights of multiple fine-tuned models improves accuracy without increasing inference time," not "without extra training" as cited.

LINK: https://proceedings.mlr.press/v162/wortsman22a.html

FOUND_TITLE: Model soups: averaging weights of multiple fine-tuned models improves accuracy without increasing inference time

FOUND_AUTHORS: Mitchell Wortsman, Gabriel Ilharco, Samir Ya Gadre, Rebecca Roelofs, Raphael Gontijo-Lopes, Ari S. Morcos, Hongseok Namkoong, Ali Farhadi, Yair Carmon, Simon Kornblith, Ludwig Schmidt

FOUND_YEAR: 2022

VERDICT: UNLIKELY
